title: Date/time: UTC storage, DateTimeUtil conversion, and a local-day range without today()
instruction: In the sandbox model, prove the utcdatetime contract. Ground it first with get_knowledge(topic="datetime-timezones"). Create: (1) a table DemoTimeEntry (label @TaxTransactionInquiry:HeaderNote, table group Main) with fields EntryId (String, EDT Num, mandatory), StartedDateTime (EDT TransDateTime) and EndedDateTime (EDT TransDateTime), plus a unique alternate-key index TimeEntryIdx on EntryId; both datetime fields hold UTC only. (2) a class DemoTimeEntryService with public static methods: (a) `void start(Num _entryId)` - inserts a row stamping StartedDateTime with DateTimeUtil::utcNow() inside ttsbegin/ttscommit; (b) `container localDayRangeUtc(date _businessDate)` - converts the LOCAL business day to a UTC range using DateTimeUtil::newDateTime plus DateTimeUtil::removeTimeZoneOffset with DateTimeUtil::getUserPreferredTimeZone(), returning [fromUtc, toUtc]; (c) `int countForBusinessDay()` - obtains the business date with systemDateGet() (NEVER today(), which is a BP error and ignores both the session date and the user time zone), takes the range from (b), and counts the rows whose StartedDateTime falls inside it with a single select count; (d) `utcdatetime displayValue(utcdatetime _stored)` - the display-edge conversion via DateTimeUtil::applyTimeZoneOffset with the user's preferred time zone. Storing a converted value in the table, or comparing across converted and stored values, FAILS the case. Both objects must build with zero BP errors.
